When generating code in this repo, follow these rules. The full conventions, architecture, and common tasks are in CLAUDE.md (read it for non-trivial work).

<!-- GENERATED by /generate-copilot from CLAUDE.md. Pre-/bootstrap: rules reflect docs/defaults.md greenfield defaults; regenerate after /bootstrap populates Conventions. -->

## Naming
- Classes PascalCase; interfaces `I` prefix; async methods `Async` suffix.
- File name matches class name exactly; one public class per file.

## Architecture
- Dependency direction inward only: API → Application → Domain. Never the reverse.
- Domain layer has zero external dependencies.

## SOLID
- DIP: every injected service is depended on through an interface (`IFoo` + `Foo`); never inject or `new` a concrete service.
- Data carriers (DTOs, entities, value objects, Options) get no interface.
- SRP: one reason to change per class; controllers stay thin and delegate immediately.
- OCP: at the third arm of a switch/if over a type code, replace with polymorphism.
- LSP: no NotImplementedException; implement the full contract or don't implement it.
- ISP: small role-based interfaces, not one fat I*Service.

## Dependency Injection
- Services scoped; factories and stateless helpers transient; caches and config singleton.
- Register via per-project extension methods, not directly in Program.cs.
- IOptions<T> for static config; IOptionsMonitor<T> for runtime-changing config.

## Data Access
- Queries live in the application/service layer, never in controllers.
- Always `.AsNoTracking()` on read-only queries.
- Repository pattern only where it adds value; don't wrap DbContext for its own sake.

## API Design
- Controllers thin — delegate to services immediately.
- Request/response DTOs separate from domain entities; never expose domain models in contracts.
- FluentValidation for request validation; no validation logic in controllers.
- Background work via BackgroundService/IHostedService; no fire-and-forget Task.Run in handlers.

## Async
- Propagate CancellationToken through every async call chain.
- No `async void`; no sync-over-async; no fire-and-forget without explicit justification.

## Null Handling
- Nullable reference types enabled; no `!` suppression without a comment explaining why.
- Guard clauses at public API boundaries only; trust internal code.

## Logging
- Structured logging only — never string interpolation in log messages.
- LoggerMessage source generators on hot paths.

## Testing
- Test naming: `MethodName_Scenario_ExpectedResult`; xUnit + NSubstitute; integration via WebApplicationFactory.
- Test behavior, not implementation; no tautological assertions; no over-mocking; never skip a failing test.
- Tests are deterministic and hermetic: no real network, clock, randomness, or order dependence.

## Boy Scout (always apply on touched files)
1. Add missing CancellationToken propagation.
2. Replace string-interpolated log messages with structured logging.
3. Add missing null checks at public boundaries.
4. Add missing `.AsNoTracking()` on read-only queries.
5. Remove unused `using` directives.
6. Remove commented-out code blocks (more than 1 line).
7. Remove unreferenced private fields, methods, or locals the compiler flags.
